notes: Qualified audit opinion due to non-compliance with PS 3280 (asset retirement obligations) - First Nation did not identify or evaluate retirement obligations for tangible capital assets as required since April 1, 2022. Comparative figures for 2023 have been restated. Prior year adjustments recorded in 2024: note 4a adds 4,423,901 and note 4b deducts 3,594,856 from accumulated surplus. Financial statements prepared under Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards (PSAS) and SAC year-end manual requirements. Business enterprises accounted for under modified equity method. Restricted cash includes: trust fund 47,953; housing 2,757,795; health 167,326; families 713,651; Fonds des générations 23,976,413; traditional activities 847,865. Net financial assets are negative (net debt) of 151,410. Accumulated surplus breakdown: unappropriated 18,285,325; appropriated 30,940,828; investment in tangible capital assets 157,733,323.
